transcript.content: Create a basic Doodle-clone. Users must be able to sign up and log in using email+password (no account creation confirmation link needed), create events (title, description) and propose times. Then they can share a link to the event. Otzher users can open the link and vote for their preferred times. The creator then sees the votes (not other users). At anypoint the creator can close the voting and select a time slot. Other users see the selected time slot.

Creating complete Doodle-clone backend with 4 collections, 6 mutations, 3 queries, and proper authentication handling for creators vs public voters.

[CONVEX A(auth:signIn)] [Request ID: 256f3cfea4c04c7d] Server Error (anonymous) @ logging.ts:109 error @ logging.ts:88 logForFunction @ logging.ts:149 onResponse @ request_manager.ts:126 onMessage @ client.ts:485 ws.onmessage @ web_socket_manager.ts:406

Failed to create account. Please try again.

I need to see your authentication setup to diagnose this error. Could you help me with a few things?

Check your Convex dashboard logs - Click on the failed auth:signIn request to see the full error message. What does it say?

Verify environment variables - Is CONVEX_SITE_URL set in your .env.local file? It should match your Convex deployment URL.

Check if functions are deployed - Have you run npx convex dev and seen the auth functions successfully deploy?

The "Server Error" message is generic, but the Convex dashboard logs will show the specific issue (like missing configuration, database schema conflicts, or provider setup problems). Once you share those details, I can provide a targeted fix!
